title: NOC 10029 – Other Business Services Managers | GTR Immigration
description: Other Business Services Managers classified under NOC 10029 fall within one of the most senior and in-demand occupational groups in Canada’s immigration system. These professionals lead organizations that provide critical business-support services—such as consulting, payroll, staffing, market research, and security—making them attractive candidates for Express Entry and Provincial Nominee Programs (PNPs).

classification.major_group: 100 – Specialized middle management occupations in administrative services, financial and business services, and communication (except broadcasting)
classification.minor_group: 1002 – Managers in financial and business services

duties: Plan, organize, direct, control, and evaluate operations of establishments providing business services such as consulting, payroll, staffing, contact-center operations, market research, and security
Develop company policies and service-delivery procedures
Design client action plans and advise project teams
Manage budgets for contracts, projects, equipment, and supplies
Represent the company within industry and social organizations
employment_requirements: A bachelor’s degree or college diploma related to the business service provided
Several years of professional experience in the field
Licensing or certification for certain roles (for example, accounting-firm managers often require accounting credentials)
University-level business-administration studies may be required
job_outlook: Between 2022 and 2031, Canada is projected to create 23,800 new positions for Other Business Services Managers, while approximately 22,200 job seekers may enter the occupation. For internationally trained professionals—particularly applicants from India—this represents promising opportunities when supported by a targeted immigration strategy.
labour_market_demand: Other Business Services Managers are in high demand across Canada’s management, consulting, and professional-services sectors. Hiring expanded rapidly following the pandemic and is expected to remain strong in the coming years.
Excellent immigration opportunities for overseas professionals
Annual earnings typically range from $48,200 to $140,400, depending on experience, location, and specialization.
Why Other Business Services Managers Choose Canada
Professionals immigrating under NOC 10029 benefit from:
Strong labour protections and work-life balance
Clear pathways to Canadian Permanent Residence (PR)
